instructions: Define the SOTA prompt boundary tags. Add the following string constants to `src/jitsu/prompts.py`: `TAG_INSTRUCTIONS`, `TAG_CONTEXT_MANIFEST`, `TAG_CONTEXT_DETAIL`, `TAG_PRIORITY_RECAP`, and `TAG_TASK_SPEC`. Their values should be the literal XML tags (e.g., `<INSTRUCTIONS>`, etc.). Do not alter any existing prompt logic yet; just define the vocabulary.
anti_patterns: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not refactor the compiler or existing prompts in this phase.
completion_criteria: The 5 XML tag constants are explicitly defined in `prompts.py`.
Code passes linting and type checking.

instructions: Hard-code the global constraints. Update `EXECUTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` in `src/jitsu/prompts.py` to exclusively contain global rules: DDD architecture adherence, 100% test coverage mandates, and 'no whole-file rewrite' policies. Strip any dynamic, phase-specific task language out of the system prompt (this belongs in the user prompt).
anti_patterns: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not put phase-specific dynamic variables (`{instructions}`) in the system prompt.
completion_criteria: `EXECUTOR_SYSTEM_PROMPT` is strictly a static, global ruleset.
Any tests verifying prompt structure are updated and passing.

instructions: Implement the SOTA U-Curve formatting. Refactor `src/jitsu/core/compiler.py` to construct the user prompt using the newly defined XML tags in this exact order: 1. `<INSTRUCTIONS>` (the phase directive), 2. `<JIT_CONTEXT_MANIFEST>` (list of resolved files), 3. `<JIT_CONTEXT_DETAIL>` (the heavy AST/file dumps), 4. `<PRIORITY_RECAP>` (a short reminder of the constraints), 5. `<TASK_AND_OUTPUT_SPEC>`. Update `tests/core/test_compiler.py` to aggressively assert this exact string ordering.
anti_patterns: STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not place the heavy `<JIT_CONTEXT_DETAIL>` at the top or bottom of the prompt. It must be in the middle (the U-Curve trough).
STRICTLY FORBIDDEN: Do not drop test coverage below 100%.
completion_criteria: The compiler outputs the exact U-Curve XML structure.
The test suite proves the ordering of the strings is mathematically correct.
